title: Detecting Disconnected Components in a Graph

statement.markdown: Given a graph represented as an adjacency list, determine the number of disconnected components in the graph.
statement.inputFormat: The input format will be a single line containing the number of vertices (n), followed by n lines describing the adjacency list for each vertex.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a single integer representing the number of disconnected components in the graph.
statement.notes: The input graph is represented as an adjacency list, where each line describes the vertices adjacent to a particular vertex.

examples.explanation: There are two disconnected components in this graph.

hints.text: Use a depth-first search (DFS) to traverse the graph and count the number of disconnected components.

editorial.title: Editorial: Finding Disconnected Components in a Graph
editorial.intuition: The key insight here is that a disconnected component in a graph is one that is not reachable from another component.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would involve checking every possible path between every pair of vertices to see if they are reachable.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach involves using a depth-first search (DFS) to traverse the graph and count the number of disconnected components.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a DFS to traverse the graph, but only traversing the graph if the current vertex has not been visited before.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The DFS approach is correct because it ensures that every connected component in the graph is visited exactly once.
editorial.dryRun: To dry run the solution, start at a random vertex and perform a DFS. If the DFS visits every vertex in the graph, then the graph has only one disconnected component.
commonMistakes: Not resetting the visited array after each test case
Not using a DFS to traverse the graph

functionSignature.java: public int solve(int n, int[][] adjList);
functionSignature.python: def solve(self, n: int, adjList: list[list[int]]) -> int;
